14	package hugolib
15	
16	import (
17		"context"
18		"errors"
19		"fmt"
20		"html/template"

35		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/common/hugo"
36		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/common/predicate"
37		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/common/types/hstring"
38		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/helpers"
39		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/markup"
40		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/markup/converter"
41		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/markup/goldmark/hugocontext"
42		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/markup/tableofcontents"
43		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/parser/metadecoders"
44		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/parser/pageparser"
45		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/resources"
46		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/resources/page"
47		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/resources/resource"
48		"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/tpl"
49		"github.com/mitchellh/mapstructure"
50		"github.com/spf13/cast"
51	)
52	
53	const (
54		internalSummaryDividerBase = "HUGOMORE42"
55	)
56	
57	var (
58		internalSummaryDividerPreString = "\n\n" + internalSummaryDividerBase + "\n\n"
59		internalSummaryDividerPre       = []byte(internalSummaryDividerPreString)
60	)
61	
62	type pageContentReplacement struct {
63		val []byte
64	
65		source pageparser.Item
66	}
67	
68	func (m *pageMetaSource) parseFrontMatter(
69		h *HugoSites,
70		sid uint64,
71	) error {
72		var filename string
73		if m.f != nil {
74			filename = m.f.Filename()
75		}
76	
77		m.pi = &contentParseInfo{
78			h:                  h,
79			sid:                sid,
80			openSource:         m.openSource,
81			shortcodeParseInfo: newShortcodeHandler(filename, h.Deps),
82		}
83	
84		source, err := m.pi.contentSource(m)
85		if err != nil {
86			return err
87		}
88	
89		items, err := pageparser.ParseBytes(
90			source,
91			pageparser.Config{
92				NoFrontMatter: m.noFrontMatter,
93			},
94		)
95		if err != nil {
96			return err
97		}
98	
99		m.pi.itemsStep1 = items
100	
101		if err := m.pi.parseSource(source, m.noFrontMatter); err != nil {
102			return err
103		}
104	
105		return nil
106	}
107	
108	func (m *pageMeta) newCachedContent(s *Site) (*cachedContent, error) {
109		if m.pageMetaSource.pi == nil {
110			panic("pageMeta.pageMetaSource.pi must be set before creating cachedContent")
111		}
112	
113		c := &cachedContent{
114			pm:          s.pageMap,
115			StaleInfo:   m,
116			pi:          m.pi,
117			enableEmoji: s.conf.EnableEmoji,
118			scopes:      hmaps.NewCache[string, *cachedContentScope](),
119		}
120		var hasName predicate.P[string] = m.pi.shortcodeParseInfo.hasName
121		c.hasShortcode.Store(&hasName)
122	
123		return c, nil
124	}
125	
126	// Content cached for a page instance.
127	type cachedContent struct {
128		pm *pageMap
129	
130		resource.StaleInfo
131	
132		// Parsed content.
133		pi *contentParseInfo
134	
135		enableEmoji bool
136	
137		// Whether the content has a given shortcode name.
138		hasShortcode atomic.Pointer[predicate.P[string]]
139	
140		scopes *hmaps.Cache[string, *cachedContentScope]
141	}
142	
143	func (c *cachedContent) getOrCreateScope(scope string, pco *pageContentOutput) *cachedContentScope {
144		key := scope + pco.po.f.Name
145		cs, _ := c.scopes.GetOrCreate(key, func() (*cachedContentScope, error) {
146			return &cachedContentScope{
147				cachedContent: c,
148				pco:           pco,
149				scope:         scope,
150			}, nil
151		})
152		return cs
153	}
154	
155	// contentParseInfo is shared between all Page instances created from the same source.
156	type contentParseInfo struct {
157		h *HugoSites
158	
159		sid uint64
160	
161		// The source bytes.
162		openSource hugio.OpenReadSeekCloser
163	
164		frontMatter map[string]any
165	
166		// Whether the parsed content contains a summary separator.
167		hasSummaryDivider bool
168	
169		// Returns the position in bytes after any front matter.
170		posMainContent int
171	
172		// Indicates whether we must do placeholder replacements.
173		hasNonMarkdownShortcode bool
174	
175		// Items from the page parser.
176		// These maps directly to the source
177		itemsStep1 pageparser.Items
178	
179		//  *shortcode, pageContentReplacement or pageparser.Item
180		itemsStep2 []any
181	
182		// The shortcode handler.
183		shortcodeParseInfo *shortcodeParseInfo
184	}
185	
186	func (pi *contentParseInfo) AddBytes(item pageparser.Item) {
187		pi.itemsStep2 = append(pi.itemsStep2, item)
188	}
189	
190	func (pi *contentParseInfo) AddReplacement(val []byte, source pageparser.Item) {
191		pi.itemsStep2 = append(pi.itemsStep2, pageContentReplacement{val: val, source: source})
192	}
193	
194	func (pi *contentParseInfo) AddShortcode(s *shortcode) {
195		pi.itemsStep2 = append(pi.itemsStep2, s)
196		if s.insertPlaceholder() {
197			pi.hasNonMarkdownShortcode = true
198		}
199	}
200	
201	// sourceMapEntry maps a range in rendered content back to a position in the original source.
202	type sourceMapEntry struct {
203		renderOffset int  // start offset in content sent to Goldmark
204		sourceOffset int  // corresponding offset in original source file
205		isShortcode  bool // if true, any pos in this segment maps to sourceOffset
206	}
207	
208	// resolveSourceOffset maps a position in rendered content to an offset in the original source.
209	func resolveSourceOffset(sm []sourceMapEntry, pos int) int {
210		i := sort.Search(len(sm), func(i int) bool {
211			return sm[i].renderOffset > pos
212		}) - 1
213		if i < 0 {
214			return pos
215		}
216		e := sm[i]
217		if e.isShortcode {
218			return e.sourceOffset
219		}
220		return e.sourceOffset + (pos - e.renderOffset)
221	}
222	
223	// contentToRenderForItems returns the content to be processed by Goldmark or similar.
224	func (pi *contentParseInfo) contentToRender(ctx context.Context, source []byte, renderedShortcodes map[string]shortcodeRenderer) ([]byte, []sourceMapEntry, bool, error) {
225		var hasVariants bool
226		c := make([]byte, 0, len(source)+(len(source)/10))
227		var sm []sourceMapEntry
228	
229		for _, it := range pi.itemsStep2 {
230			switch v := it.(type) {
231			case pageparser.Item:

236				c = append(c, v.val...)
237			case *shortcode:
238				sm = append(sm, sourceMapEntry{renderOffset: len(c), sourceOffset: v.pos, isShortcode: true})
239				if !v.insertPlaceholder() {

242					if !found {
243						// This should never happen.
244						panic(fmt.Sprintf("rendered shortcode %q not found", v.placeholder))
245					}

247					b, more, err := renderedShortcode.renderShortcode(ctx)
248					if err != nil {
249						return nil, nil, false, fmt.Errorf("failed to render shortcode: %w", err)
250					}
251					hasVariants = hasVariants || more
252					c = append(c, []byte(b)...)
253	
254				} else {
255					// Insert the placeholder so we can insert the content after
256					// markdown processing.
257					c = append(c, []byte(v.placeholder)...)
258				}
259			default:

80	// InnerDeindent returns the (potentially de-indented) inner content of the shortcode.
81	func (scp *ShortcodeWithPage) InnerDeindent() template.HTML {
82		if scp.indentation == "" {
83			return scp.Inner
84		}
85		scp.innerDeindentInit.Do(func() {
86			b := bp.GetBuffer()
87			text.VisitLinesAfter(string(scp.Inner), func(s string) {
88				if after, ok := strings.CutPrefix(s, scp.indentation); ok {
89					b.WriteString(after)
90				} else {
91					b.WriteString(s)
92				}
93			})
94			scp.innerDeindent = template.HTML(b.String())
95			bp.PutBuffer(b)
96		})
97	
98		return scp.innerDeindent
99	}

144	// Get is a convenience method to look up shortcode parameters by its key.
145	func (scp *ShortcodeWithPage) Get(key any) any {
146		if scp.Params == nil {
147			return nil
148		}
149		if reflect.ValueOf(scp.Params).Len() == 0 {
150			return nil
151		}
152	
153		var x reflect.Value
154	
155		switch key.(type) {
156		case int64, int32, int16, int8, int:
157			if reflect.TypeOf(scp.Params).Kind() == reflect.Map {
158				// We treat this as a non error, so people can do similar to
159				// {{ $myParam := .Get "myParam" | default .Get 0 }}
160				// Without having to do additional checks.
161				return nil
162			} else if reflect.TypeOf(scp.Params).Kind() == reflect.Slice {
163				idx := int(reflect.ValueOf(key).Int())
164				ln := reflect.ValueOf(scp.Params).Len()
165				if idx > ln-1 {
166					return ""
167				}
168				x = reflect.ValueOf(scp.Params).Index(idx)
169			}
170		case string:
171			if reflect.TypeOf(scp.Params).Kind() == reflect.Map {
172				x = reflect.ValueOf(scp.Params).MapIndex(reflect.ValueOf(key))
173				if !x.IsValid() {
174					return ""
175				}
176			} else if reflect.TypeOf(scp.Params).Kind() == reflect.Slice {
177				// We treat this as a non error, so people can do similar to
178				// {{ $myParam := .Get "myParam" | default .Get 0 }}
179				// Without having to do additional checks.
180				return nil
181			}
182		}
183	
184		return x.Interface()
185	}

content.text: I found the likely wrapping point: `RenderShortcodes` wraps nested output whenever `IsInGoldmark` is set, but standalone `.RenderShortcodes` can inherit that flag from a Markdown shortcode even though no later Goldmark render will consume and strip the wrappers.

RenderShortcodes: {{ .RenderShortcodes }}|

{{ $p := site.GetPage "includeme" }}
RenderShortcodes2: {{ $p.RenderShortcodes }}|

title: "p1"
---
{{% headings "headings" %}}
-- content/includeme.md --
---
title: "includeme"
---
## Heading 2
`

	b := Test(t, files)

	b.AssertNoRenderShortcodesArtifacts()
}

func TestRenderShortcodesStandalone(t *testing.T) {
	t.Parallel()

	files := `
-- hugo.toml --
disableKinds = ['section','rss','sitemap','taxonomy','term']
[outputs]
page = ['md']
[mediaTypes.'text/markdown']
suffixes = ['md']
[outputFormats.md]
mediaType = 'text/markdown'
-- layouts/all.md --
RenderShortcodes: {{ .RenderShortcodes }}|
-- layouts/_shortcodes/headings.html --
## Heading 1
### Heading 2
{{ $p := site.GetPage "includeme" }}
RenderShortcodes2: {{ $p.RenderShortcodes }}|
-- content/p1.md --
title: "p1"
---
{{% headings "headings" %}}
-- content/includeme.md --
---
title: "includeme"
---
## Heading 2
`

	b := Test(t, files)

	b.AssertNoRenderShortcodesArtifacts()
}

content.text:
Implemented the fix without committing.

Summary:
- Added handling so nested page context markers are only applied when `.RenderShortcodes` output is actually going back through Goldmark/content rendering.
- For standalone `.RenderShortcodes` calls in shortcode/template execution, any nested Hugo context wrappers are stripped instead of being emitted.
- Added `hugocontext.Unwrap` helper and used it from `cachedContentScope.RenderShortcodes`.

Validation:
- Temporarily added a regression test reproducing the standalone Markdown output issue.
- Confirmed it failed before the fix and passed after.
- Removed the temporary test as requested.
- Ran focused RenderShortcodes tests successfully:

```sh
go test ./hugolib -run 'TestRenderShortcodes(Basic|NestedPageContextIssue12356|WithHTML|IncludeMarkdownFileWithoutTrailingNewline)$' -count=1
```

Changed files:
- `hugolib/page__content.go`
- `markup/goldmark/hugocontext/hugocontext.go`
